description: Determine the runaway rotational speed (RPM) of a wind turbine rotor. Generate a NACA 4412 airfoil and a polar for Re=1,000,000 with a range of -5 to 25 degrees. Design a 3-bladed rotor with a 6m radius, 0.5m hub radius, constant 0.5m chord, and 0 twist using the NACA 4412 polar. Perform a BEM Rotor Simulation at a fixed wind speed of 15 m/s and 0-degree pitch, utilizing an RPM sweep to identify the runaway RPM where aerodynamic power drops to zero. Export the Power vs RPM simulation graph data to /home/ga/Documents/projects/runaway_data.txt. Record the identified runaway RPM in /home/ga/Documents/projects/runaway_report.txt in the format 'Runaway_RPM: <value>' and save the project to /home/ga/Documents/projects/safety_analysis.wpa.
